task_completion.description: Verifies that a 3D surface for the bone structure has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius GUI, the 'Surfaces' panel lists a surface (often named 'Bone' or similar), and a 3D skull is visible in the 3D viewer pane.

task_completion.description: Verifies that a 3D surface for the skin/soft tissue has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Surfaces' panel lists a second surface (often named 'Skin', 'Tissue', or similar) alongside the bone surface.

task_completion.description: Verifies the bone surface is fully opaque.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The transparency slider or value for the bone surface in the surface properties is set to 0 (or < 0.1), and the bone appears completely solid in the 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: Verifies the skin surface is semi-transparent, allowing the bone to be seen inside.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The transparency slider/value for the skin surface is set between 0.2 and 0.9. In the 3D viewer, the outer skin layer is translucent, and the inner bone structure is clearly visible through it.

task_completion.description: Verifies the project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project As' dialog shows the filename 'skin_bone_overlay.inv3' being saved in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory, or the main InVesalius window title updates to show this filename.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent interacted with the InVesalius 3 GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through InVesalius menus, surface creation tools, and transparency sliders.

integrity.description: Verifies the 3D model is genuinely rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer shows the actual CT Cranium data being manipulated, with appropriate lighting and rendering artifacts typical of InVesalius, not a static pasted image.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't just use a terminal script to create a fake .inv3 file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running python scripts or echo commands to bypass the GUI requirements.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/Documents/skin_bone_overlay.inv3'. The visualization requires at least 2 surfaces: one opaque (transparency < 0.1) representing the bone, and one semi-transparent (transparency between 0.2 and 0.9) representing the skin/soft-tissue.
